title: What Is Collective Behavior in Biology?
summary: A short note on how local rules, signaling, and physical interactions generate coordinated behavior across cells, microbes, and tissues.

title: Noise as a Biological Control Parameter
summary: A note on why stochasticity is not only a nuisance, but also a mechanism for switching, exploration, and flexible regulation.

title: Reaction-Diffusion Intuition
summary: A conceptual guide to excitable media, activator-inhibitor dynamics, and why simple local rules can create waves and patterns.

title: Quorum Sensing as Distributed Computation
summary: A note connecting bacterial communication, threshold logic, and population-scale decision-making in spatially extended systems.
